input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install latest .NET SDK (includes MAUI workload support)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Discover & Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads (find exact maui ID)
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (pulls Android, Windows SDK dependencies)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Android SDK Components

The MAUI workload installs a base Android SDK, but you may need to manage versions:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was installed
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
# Or check:
dotnet android sdk info

# List installed/available Android platforms dynamically
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install specific platform (discover latest stable first)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 4. Set Up Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available system images
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "system-images"

# Install a system image
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Verify
& "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -list-avds
```

## 5. Enable Windows App SDK (WinUI 3 target)

```powershell
# Install Windows App SDK runtime (or rely on VS build tools)
winget search "Windows App SDK"
winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK.1.6

# Enable Developer Mode (required for sideloading)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d 1
```

## 6. (Optional) Install Visual Studio Build Tools

If you want full IDE support or need MSBuild:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop --includeRecommended --passive"
```

## 7. Create & Run a Test Project

```powershell
# Discover available MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create project
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

## 8. Discover Target Framework Versions Dynamically

```powershell
# See which TFMs your SDK supports
dotnet --info | Select-String "Microsoft.Android|Microsoft.Windows"

# Check installed Android workload versions
dotnet workload list --versions

# Find supported Windows TFM from SDK
ls "$env:ProgramFiles\dotnet\packs" | Where-Object { $_.Name -like "*Windows*" }
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0` with your installed SDK major version if different.
- Android platform numbers (e.g., `android-35`) change yearly — always use `sdkmanager --list` to discover current versions.
- Run the `reg add` command in an elevated (Admin) terminal.
